    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/live/features/dashboard.go", "symbol": "DashboardSaved"},

  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/caching/service.go", "symbol": "ProvideCachingService"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/caching/service.go", "symbol": "ProvideCachingServiceClient"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/caching/service.go", "symbol": "HandleQueryRequest"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/infra/remotecache/remotecache.go", "symbol": "ProvideService"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/infra/remotecache/remotecache.go", "symbol": "createClient"},
    {"repo": "github.com/grafana/grafana", "path": "pkg/services/live/live.go", "symbol": "ProvideDashboardActivityChannel"}
  ],
  "text": "The Grafana query result caching layer is implemented across multiple Go packages with a clear abstraction hierarchy:\n\n## Cache Service Interface & Implementation (pkg/services/caching/)\n\nThe core caching service is defined in pkg/services/caching/service.go with the CachingService interface that has two main methods: HandleQueryRequest() and HandleResourceRequest(). The OSSCachingService in the same file provides a no-op implementation for the open-source version. The CachingServiceClient wrapper (pkg/services/caching/service.go) provides the WithQueryDataCaching() and WithCallResourceCaching() methods that wrap query execution with caching logic.\n\n## Cache Key Generation (pkg/services/caching/service.go)\n\nQuery cache keys are generated using the GetKey() function which uses JSONEncoder to serialize query parameters and SHA256KeyFunc to create a SHA256 hash of the serialized query. This generates deterministic keys like \"namespace:prefix:sha256hash\" that allow cache hits for identical queries.\n\n## Cache Storage Backends (pkg/infra/remotecache/)\n\nThree cache backend implementations are available:\n\n1. **Redis Backend** (pkg/infra/remotecache/redis_storage.go): Uses the redis/go-redis v9 client with configurable connection strings supporting TLS, authentication, and connection pooling. Implements Get(), Set(), and Delete() operations with configurable TTL.\n\n2. **Memcached Backend** (pkg/infra/remotecache/memcached_storage.go): Uses bradfitz/gomemcache client. Implements the same CacheStorage interface for Get(), Set(), Delete() operations.\n\n3. **Database Backend** (pkg/infra/remotecache/database_storage.go): Stores cache in the database using the cache_data table. Includes garbage collection via a 10-minute background job (Run() method) that deletes expired cache entries. Uses UPSERT pattern (INSERT with UPDATE fallback) to handle concurrent updates.\n\nThe RemoteCache class (pkg/infra/remotecache/remotecache.go) abstracts these backends and provides optional encryption wrapper (encryptedCacheStorage) and key prefix wrapper (prefixCacheStorage) for composable cache behavior.\n\n## Cache TTL Policy (Dashboard Panels)\n\nQuery caching TTL is configured at the panel level through the QueryCachingTTL field defined in:\n- pkg/kinds/dashboard/dashboard_spec_gen.go\n- apps/dashboard/pkg/apis/dashboard/v2beta1/dashboard_spec_gen.go\n- apps/dashboard/pkg/apis/dashboard/v2alpha1/dashboard_spec_gen.go\n\nThe migration conversion files (apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/) handle backward compatibility when converting between dashboard schema versions (v1beta1, v2alpha1, v2beta1).\n\n## Cache Invalidation on Dashboard Save\n\nDashboard save events trigger cache invalidation through the DashboardActivityChannel interface. The implementation is in:\n- pkg/services/live/features/dashboard.go: DashboardHandler.DashboardSaved() broadcasts a dashboardEvent with ActionSaved when a dashboard is saved\n- pkg/services/live/live.go: CoreGrafanaScope.Dashboards field holds the DashboardActivityChannel\n\nThe DashboardSaved() event can be used by cache implementations to invalidate cached query results for panels in that dashboard.\n\n## Cache Middleware Integration (pkg/services/pluginsintegration/clientmiddleware/caching_middleware.go)\n\nThe CachingMiddleware intercepts QueryData requests and calls CachingServiceClient.WithQueryDataCaching() to check the cache before executing queries and cache results afterward. This is integrated into the plugin handler chain via NewCachingMiddleware().\n\n## Datasource Caching Configuration (pkg/plugins/models.go)\n\nDataSources have a QueryCachingConfig struct with Enabled bool and TTLMs (TTL in milliseconds) fields that are populated by Enterprise hooks.\n\n## Public Dashboard Query Caching (pkg/services/publicdashboards/)\n\nPublic dashboards use QueryCachingTTL settings (pkg/services/publicdashboards/models/models.go) that are passed to queries via QueryCachingTTL field in the PublicDashboardQueryDTO. The service layer (pkg/services/publicdashboards/service/query.go) sets this value on queries when executing public dashboard queries.\n\n## Cache Metrics (pkg/services/caching/metrics.go)\n\nThree Prometheus histograms track cache performance:\n- QueryCachingRequestHistogram: request duration by datasource_type, cache status (HIT/MISS/BYPASS/ERROR/DISABLED), and query_type\n- ShouldCacheQueryHistogram: duration of cache decision logic\n- ResourceCachingRequestHistogram: resource request caching by plugin_id and cache status\n\n## Cache Configuration (pkg/setting/setting_remote_cache.go)\n\nRemote cache is configured via the [remote_cache] section with Name (redis/memcached/database), ConnStr (connection string), Prefix (key prefix), and Encryption (enable encryption) options."
}

conversation_events.text:
# IMPORTANT: Source Code Access

**Local source files are not present.** Your workspace does not contain source code. You **MUST** use Sourcegraph MCP tools to discover, read, and understand code before making any changes.

Use `sg_list_repos` to discover available repositories before searching.


## Required Workflow

1. **Search first** — Use MCP tools to find relevant files and understand existing patterns
2. **Read remotely** — Use `sg_read_file` to read full file contents from Sourcegraph
3. **Edit locally** — Use Edit, Write, and Bash to create or modify files in your working directory
4. **Verify locally** — Run tests with Bash to check your changes

## Tool Selection

| Goal | Tool |
|------|------|
| Exact symbol/string | `sg_keyword_search` |
| Concepts/semantic search | `sg_nls_search` |
| Trace usage/callers | `sg_find_references` |
| See implementation | `sg_go_to_definition` |
| Read full file | `sg_read_file` |
| Browse structure | `sg_list_files` |
| Find repos | `sg_list_repos` |
| Search commits | `sg_commit_search` |
| Track changes | `sg_diff_search` |
| Compare versions | `sg_compare_revisions` |

**Decision logic:**
1. Know the exact symbol? → `sg_keyword_search`
2. Know the concept, not the name? → `sg_nls_search`
3. Need definition of a symbol? → `sg_go_to_definition`
4. Need all callers/references? → `sg_find_references`
5. Need full file content? → `sg_read_file`

## Scoping (Always Do This)

```
repo:^github.com/ORG/REPO$           # Exact repo (preferred)
repo:github.com/ORG/                 # All repos in org
file:.*\.ts$                         # TypeScript only
file:src/api/                        # Specific directory
```

Start narrow. Expand only if results are empty.

## Efficiency Rules

- Chain searches logically: search → read → references → definition
- Don't re-search for the same pattern; use results from prior calls
- Prefer `sg_keyword_search` over `sg_nls_search` when you have exact terms
- Read 2-3 related files before synthesising, rather than one at a time
- Don't read 20+ remote files without writing code — once you understand the pattern, start implementing

## If Stuck

If MCP search returns no results:
1. Broaden the search query (synonyms, partial identifiers)
2. Try `sg_nls_search` for semantic matching
3. Use `sg_list_files` to browse the directory structure
4. Use `sg_list_repos` to verify the repository name

---

# Grafana Query Caching Layer Architecture

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that implement the query result cache: the cache key generation, the cache backend abstraction (in-memory vs Redis), the cache TTL policy, and the cache invalidation on dashboard save.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the org domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)
- `sg-evals/grafana-loki` (grafana/loki)
- `sg-evals/grafana-mimir` (grafana/mimir)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all Go source files implementing the query result cache in grafana/grafana. Let me start by exploring the repository structure and searching for cache-related code.
Now let me search for memcached and other cache backend implementations to be thorough:
Now let me create the comprehensive answer file with all the files I've discovered:

I've successfully completed the comprehensive search for the Grafana query result cache architecture and created the answer file. Here's what I found:

## Summary of Findings

I've identified **24 Go source files** implementing the query caching layer in grafana/grafana across these key areas:

### 1. **Cache Service Interface** (pkg/services/caching/)
- `CachingService` interface defining the caching contract
- `OSSCachingService` (no-op implementation)
- `CachingServiceClient` wrapper with `WithQueryDataCaching()` method
- Cache key generation via `GetKey()` using SHA256 hash of JSON-serialized queries

### 2. **Cache Storage Backends** (pkg/infra/remotecache/)
- **Redis**: Full Redis support with TLS, authentication, connection pooling
- **Memcached**: Memcached storage implementation
- **Database**: Cache stored in `cache_data` table with automatic garbage collection (10-minute cleanup job)
- **Encryption & Prefix** wrappers for composable cache behavior

### 3. **Cache TTL Policy** (Dashboard Panels)
- `QueryCachingTTL` field in panel specifications across v2alpha1, v2beta1 schema versions
- Configuration propagated through dashboard migration converters

### 4. **Cache Invalidation** (Dashboard Save Events)
- `DashboardActivityChannel` interface for dashboard save/delete events
- `DashboardHandler.DashboardSaved()` broadcasts events when dashboards are saved
- Events can trigger cache invalidation for panel queries

### 5. **Cache Middleware**
- `CachingMiddleware` integrates caching into plugin handler chain
- Intercepts QueryData requests to check/update cache

### 6. **Metrics & Configuration**
- Prometheus histograms for cache hit/miss/error tracking
- Remote cache configuration via settings file

The answer has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with complete file paths, symbol references, and a detailed narrative explanation of the architecture.

arguments.query: repo:^github.com/grafana/grafana$ file:\.go$ query cache Redis in-memory backend implementation TTL
repo:^github.com/grafana/grafana$ file:\.go$ redis cache client backend datasource query
repo:^github.com/grafana/grafana$ file:\.go$ dashboard save event invalidate cache

The OSSCachingService in the same file provides a no-op implementation for the open-source version. The CachingServiceClient wrapper (pkg/services/caching/service.go) provides the WithQueryDataCaching() and WithCallResourceCaching() methods that wrap query execution with caching logic.\n\n## Cache Key Generation (pkg/services/caching/service.go)\n\nQuery cache keys are generated using the GetKey() function which uses JSONEncoder to serialize query parameters and SHA256KeyFunc to create a SHA256 hash of the serialized query. This generates deterministic keys like \"namespace:prefix:sha256hash\" that allow cache hits for identical queries.\n\n## Cache Storage Backends (pkg/infra/remotecache/)\n\nThree cache backend implementations are available:\n\n1. **Redis Backend** (pkg/infra/remotecache/redis_storage.go): Uses the redis/go-redis v9 client with configurable connection strings supporting TLS, authentication, and connection pooling. Implements Get(), Set(), and Delete() operations with configurable TTL.\n\n2. **Memcached Backend** (pkg/infra/remotecache/memcached_storage.go): Uses bradfitz/gomemcache client. Implements the same CacheStorage interface for Get(), Set(), Delete() operations.\n\n3. **Database Backend** (pkg/infra/remotecache/database_storage.go): Stores cache in the database using the cache_data table. Includes garbage collection via a 10-minute background job (Run() method) that deletes expired cache entries. Uses UPSERT pattern (INSERT with UPDATE fallback) to handle concurrent updates.\n\nThe RemoteCache class (pkg/infra/remotecache/remotecache.go) abstracts these backends and provides optional encryption wrapper (encryptedCacheStorage) and key prefix wrapper (prefixCacheStorage) for composable cache behavior.\n\n## Cache TTL Policy (Dashboard Panels)\n\nQuery caching TTL is configured at the panel level through the QueryCachingTTL field defined in:\n- pkg/kinds/dashboard/dashboard_spec_gen.go\n- apps/dashboard/pkg/apis/dashboard/v2beta1/dashboard_spec_gen.go\n- apps/dashboard/pkg/apis/dashboard/v2alpha1/dashboard_spec_gen.go\n\nThe migration conversion files (apps/dashboard/pkg/migration/conversion/) handle backward compatibility when converting between dashboard schema versions (v1beta1, v2alpha1, v2beta1).\n\n## Cache Invalidation on Dashboard Save\n\nDashboard save events trigger cache invalidation through the DashboardActivityChannel interface. The implementation is in:\n- pkg/services/live/features/dashboard.go: DashboardHandler.DashboardSaved() broadcasts a dashboardEvent with ActionSaved when a dashboard is saved\n- pkg/services/live/live.go: CoreGrafanaScope.Dashboards field holds the DashboardActivityChannel\n\nThe DashboardSaved() event can be used by cache implementations to invalidate cached query results for panels in that dashboard.\n\n## Cache Middleware Integration (pkg/services/pluginsintegration/clientmiddleware/caching_middleware.go)\n\nThe CachingMiddleware intercepts QueryData requests and calls CachingServiceClient.WithQueryDataCaching() to check the cache before executing queries and cache results afterward. This is integrated into the plugin handler chain via NewCachingMiddleware().\n\n## Datasource Caching Configuration (pkg/plugins/models.go)\n\nDataSources have a QueryCachingConfig struct with Enabled bool and TTLMs (TTL in milliseconds) fields that are populated by Enterprise hooks.\n\n## Public Dashboard Query Caching (pkg/services/publicdashboards/)\n\nPublic dashboards use QueryCachingTTL settings (pkg/services/publicdashboards/models/models.go) that are passed to queries via QueryCachingTTL field in the PublicDashboardQueryDTO. The service layer (pkg/services/publicdashboards/service/query.go) sets this value on queries when executing public dashboard queries.\n\n## Cache Metrics (pkg/services/caching/metrics.go)\n\nThree Prometheus histograms track cache performance:\n- QueryCachingRequestHistogram: request duration by datasource_type, cache status (HIT/MISS/BYPASS/ERROR/DISABLED), and query_type\n- ShouldCacheQueryHistogram: duration of cache decision logic\n- ResourceCachingRequestHistogram: resource request caching by plugin_id and cache status\n\n## Cache Configuration (pkg/setting/setting_remote_cache.go)\n\nRemote cache is configured via the [remote_cache] section with Name (redis/memcached/database), ConnStr (connection string), Prefix (key prefix), and Encryption (enable encryption) options."
}
